title: US Military Strikes Iranian Drone and Missile Sites After Attack on Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
summary: US forces conducted strikes against Iranian drone and missile sites following an IRGC attack on a cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. The International Maritime Organization (IMO) temporarily paused evacuation operations in the strait amid the escalating conflict. Iran's IRGC reasserted control over the Strait of Hormuz and warned Gulf states against backing alternative shipping routes. At least two tankers reversed course after the IRGC rejected a new Hormuz evacuation route proposed by the IMO.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a critical global oil transit chokepoint — nearly 20% of world oil trade passes through it — making this conflict directly relevant to energy security, international maritime law, and US-Iran geopolitics. Questions on chokepoints, freedom of navigation, and West Asia conflict are perennial UPSC themes.
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: handles ~20% of global oil trade; located between Iran and Oman
IRGC (Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps) attacked a cargo ship near Oman
IMO paused evacuation operations in the strait
US struck Iranian drone and missile sites in retaliation

title: Israel and Lebanon Sign Framework Peace Deal After US-Mediated Talks
summary: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework peace deal brokered through US-mediated negotiations. The agreement marks a significant diplomatic development in West Asia, following months of cross-border hostilities. Details of the framework terms are being formalised through ongoing diplomatic channels.
exam_angle: Israel-Lebanon relations, the role of the US as a mediator in West Asian conflicts, and the geopolitics of the region are standard UPSC IR topics. This deal connects to themes of bilateral peace processes, UN Security Council resolutions on Lebanon (e.g., UNSC 1701), and regional stability.
key_facts: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework peace deal
Deal mediated by the United States
Ends prolonged cross-border hostilities between the two countries
Significant diplomatic event in West Asia geopolitics

title: Canada Officially Acknowledges 1985 Air India Kanishka Bombing as Khalistani Extremist Attack
summary: Canada has formally admitted that the 1985 Air India Flight 182 (Kanishka) bombing was carried out by Khalistani extremists, calling it a 'heinous act of terror.' The bombing killed 329 people and remains the deadliest aviation terrorist attack in Canadian history. This acknowledgment comes 40 years after the incident, at a time of heightened India-Canada tensions over Khalistani activities on Canadian soil. India had long maintained that Canada was not doing enough to address Khalistani extremism.
exam_angle: India-Canada relations and the issue of Khalistani extremism are directly relevant to GS Paper 2 IR. This admission is significant in the context of diplomatic friction between India and Canada over designated terrorists operating freely in Canada. Connects to India's foreign policy doctrine of zero tolerance for cross-border terrorism.
key_facts: Air India Flight 182 (Kanishka) bombed on June 23, 1985
329 people killed — deadliest aviation terror attack in Canadian history
Canada formally acknowledged Khalistani extremists carried out the attack
Incident occurred 40 years ago; acknowledgment comes amid India-Canada diplomatic tensions

title: India Loses Bangladesh Matarbari Port Project to China; Beijing-Dhaka Announce 'Community with Shared Future'
summary: India has reportedly lost a key port development project in Bangladesh to China. China and Bangladesh announced plans to build a 'community with shared future in a new era,' signalling deepening bilateral ties under the Muhammad Yunus-led interim government in Dhaka. China's Foreign Ministry stated its ties with Bangladesh do not target any third party. The development is seen as a significant setback for India's connectivity and strategic interests in its immediate neighbourhood.
exam_angle: India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy, the strategic importance of Bangladesh for India's North-East connectivity, and China's growing influence in South Asia through port diplomacy (String of Pearls) are core UPSC IR themes. Matarbari deep-sea port is significant for Bay of Bengal access.
key_facts: India reportedly lost the Matarbari deep-sea port project in Bangladesh to China
China-Bangladesh announced 'community with shared future in a new era'
Bangladesh governed by Muhammad Yunus-led interim government since mid-2024
Relevant to India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and Bay of Bengal geopolitics

title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement: Modern Trade Partnership
summary: India and New Zealand are working towards a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) described as a 'modern trade partnership,' with discussions covering goods, services, and investment. The FTA is part of India's broader push to conclude trade deals with developed economies. India has recently concluded the India-UK Comprehensive Economic Trade Agreement (CETA) and is pursuing deals with other partners including the US.
exam_angle: India's FTA strategy is a key GS 3 topic, covering trade policy, balance of payments, and economic diplomacy. Linking this to the India-UK CETA (signed recently) and the India-US trade negotiations provides a comprehensive picture of India's trade diplomacy.
key_facts: India-New Zealand FTA negotiations ongoing
Described as a 'modern trade partnership' covering goods, services, and investment
Part of India's broader FTA diplomacy alongside India-UK CETA
Source: The Hindu

title: India to Deploy 1,000 Trade Advisers Ahead of India-UK CETA to Help Exporters
summary: India plans to deploy 1,000 trade advisers to help Indian exporters leverage the recently concluded India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA). The Apparel Export Promotion Council (AEPC) has noted that the India-UK trade agreement will offer better market access to Indian garment exporters. This deployment is part of the government's effort to maximise export benefits from the landmark bilateral trade deal.
exam_angle: The India-UK CETA is a landmark trade agreement and a major GS 3 (Economy) topic. Understanding its implementation mechanisms, sectors benefiting from it (textiles/garments), and the role of export promotion bodies like AEPC is exam-relevant.
key_facts: 1,000 trade advisers to be deployed to help exporters use India-UK CETA
AEPC (Apparel Export Promotion Council) highlighted better market access for garment exporters
India-UK CETA is a Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement
Garments/textiles is a key sector benefiting from the deal
source: The New Indian Express / The Hindu

title: Trump Administration Warns of Return to Higher Tariffs Amid India-US Trade Deal Talks
summary: The Trump administration issued a fresh warning that old, higher tariff rates may return for countries that do not conclude trade deals with the US. This warning comes amid ongoing India-US trade negotiations. Separately, Trump threatened 100% tariffs on European countries over digital services taxes. The uncertainty affects India's export calculations, particularly as it is among the countries in active trade negotiations with Washington.
exam_angle: India-US trade negotiations, reciprocal tariffs, and global trade policy under the Trump administration are highly relevant for GS 3 (Economy) and GS 2 (IR). The tariff threat mechanism connects to WTO rules, MFN obligations, and India's export strategy.
key_facts: Trump administration warned old higher tariffs may return if trade deals not concluded
India-US bilateral trade negotiations ongoing
Trump separately threatened 100% tariffs on EU over digital services taxes
Affects India's export strategy and trade policy planning

title: EY Projects India GDP Growth at Up to 6.8% in FY2026-27 on Resilient Domestic Demand
summary: Global consultancy EY (Ernst & Young) has projected India's economy may grow up to 6.8% in FY2026-27, driven by resilient domestic demand. The report highlights that while global headwinds persist, India's consumption and investment cycle remain relatively robust. This projection is broadly aligned with IMF and RBI forecasts for the Indian economy.
exam_angle: GDP growth projections by credible agencies are standard data points for GS 3 (Economy). Aspirants should know multiple agency projections (IMF, World Bank, RBI, ADB, EY) for India's GDP growth for the current fiscal year.
key_facts: EY projects India GDP growth at up to 6.8% for FY2026-27
Growth driven by resilient domestic demand
EY = Ernst & Young, a global consultancy firm
Aligns broadly with IMF and RBI projections for India

title: India Startup Funding Tops $1 Billion in a Week; CRED Mega-Round Powers Rebound
summary: India's startup ecosystem recorded over $1 billion in funding in a single week, with a mega funding round by fintech company CRED being the major driver. The development signals a sharp rebound in startup investment sentiment. India's startup ecosystem continues to be among the world's largest by number of unicorns and funding volumes.
exam_angle: India's startup ecosystem, unicorn count, and funding environment are relevant for GS 3 (Economy) topics on entrepreneurship, digital economy, and the Startup India initiative. CRED is a notable Indian fintech unicorn.
key_facts: India startup funding exceeded $1 billion in one week
CRED (fintech company) led with a mega funding round
Marks a sharp weekly rebound in startup investment
India is among the world's top startup ecosystems by unicorn count

title: Operation Sindoor: Names of 6 Soldiers Killed in Action Released for First Time
summary: The Indian government publicly released the names of six soldiers who were killed in action during Operation Sindoor for the first time. Operation Sindoor was India's military operation conducted in response to the April 2025 Pahalgam terror attack, targeting terrorist infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir. The release of names is part of official recognition of the soldiers' sacrifice.
exam_angle: Operation Sindoor is a landmark event in India's recent defence and security history. Questions on India's counter-terrorism doctrine, civil-military relations, and the Pahalgam attack connect directly to this. Aspirants must know the context, objectives, and outcomes of Operation Sindoor.
key_facts: 6 soldiers killed in action during Operation Sindoor; names released officially for first time
Operation Sindoor: India's military strikes on terrorist infrastructure in Pakistan/PoK
Conducted in response to the April 2025 Pahalgam terror attack
Release of names = official government recognition of martyrdom

title: OpenAI Appoints Prabhjeet Singh (Ex-Uber India Head) as Managing Director for India
summary: OpenAI has appointed Prabhjeet Singh, former head of Uber India, as its Managing Director for India. Singh is an IIT-IIM alumnus. His appointment marks a significant expansion of OpenAI's operations in India, which is one of the fastest-growing AI markets globally. Singh will lead OpenAI's India business, partnerships, and growth strategy.
exam_angle: OpenAI's expansion into India, AI governance, and the role of global AI companies in India's digital economy are relevant for GS 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS 2 (Governance). This also connects to India's AI Mission and the government's push for AI adoption.
key_facts: OpenAI appoints Prabhjeet Singh as Managing Director for India
Singh is former head of Uber India and an IIT-IIM alumnus
OpenAI is the company behind ChatGPT and GPT series of AI models
Appointment signals OpenAI's strategic expansion in Indian market

title: OpenAI Restricts New AI Models to 'Trusted Partners' at US Government Request; China's Zhipu Gains Ground
summary: OpenAI has limited the release of its new AI models to 'trusted partners' following a request from the US government, raising concerns about global AI access. Separately, Anthropic is negotiating a deal with the US administration to lift restrictions on its AI models. China's Zhipu AI is reportedly closing the gap with top US AI models as American companies face export restrictions. The US government also assured India of stable AI technology access following the Anthropic model suspension.
exam_angle: AI export controls, US-China tech competition, and India's access to AI technologies are highly relevant for GS 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS 2 (IR). The intersection of AI governance, national security, and technology diplomacy is an emerging exam theme.
key_facts: OpenAI restricted new AI model releases to 'trusted partners' at US government request
Anthropic negotiating deal with US to lift AI model export curbs
US assured India of stable AI technology access
China's Zhipu AI gaining ground as US AI firms face government restrictions
source: CNBC / Bloomberg / The New Indian Express

title: 25% of Indian Tech Services Firms Have Moved AI Experiments to Production Level: Nasscom
summary: According to Nasscom, 25% of Indian technology services companies have moved their artificial intelligence experiments from pilot/proof-of-concept stage to production-level deployment. This marks a significant maturation of AI adoption in India's IT sector. India's IT sector, dominated by companies like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro, is a major driver of the country's services exports.
exam_angle: Nasscom data on India's IT sector and AI adoption is relevant for GS 3 (Sci-Tech and Economy). Nasscom is the premier industry body for India's IT-BPM sector; its reports are frequently cited in exams as evidence of India's digital economy growth.
key_facts: 25% of Indian tech services firms have moved AI experiments to production level
Source: Nasscom (National Association of Software and Service Companies)
Nasscom is India's apex IT-BPM industry body
Indicates significant maturation of enterprise AI adoption in India

title: Ram Mandir Donation Row: 8 Arrested Including Attendant and Ex-Bank Staff; Champat Rai at Centre
summary: Eight people have been arrested in connection with a donation misappropriation row at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. Those arrested include a temple attendant and former bank staff. Champat Rai, General Secretary of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust and an RSS pracharak, is at the centre of the controversy. The case raises questions about financial management and transparency of the trust that oversees one of India's most prominent religious sites.
exam_angle: The Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust and Ayodhya Ram Temple are significant from a cultural and polity perspective. Questions on religious trusts, their governance, and Article 26 (right of religious denominations to manage their own affairs) can be linked. The Champat Rai angle connects to the RSS and trust governance.
key_facts: 8 persons arrested in Ram Temple (Ayodhya) donation misappropriation case
Champat Rai is General Secretary of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust
Champat Rai is also an RSS pracharak
Case involves alleged misappropriation of temple donations/offerings

title: India Tops ISSF Junior World Championship Medal Tally in Germany
summary: India's junior shooters topped the overall medal tally at the ISSF (International Shooting Sport Federation) Junior World Championship held in Germany. This is a significant achievement for India's shooting sports programme, which has consistently produced world-class talent at the junior level. India's shooting contingent has been a major medal earner at international events in recent years.
exam_angle: India's performance at international sporting championships, particularly in shooting, is relevant for persons-in-news and sports diplomacy sections. The ISSF is the international governing body for Olympic shooting sports. Indian shooting has been a focus area since Abhinav Bindra's 2008 Olympic gold.
key_facts: India topped the medal tally at ISSF Junior World Championship
Championship held in Germany
ISSF = International Shooting Sport Federation (Olympic shooting's governing body)
India has been a consistent top performer in international shooting competitions

title: Venezuela Earthquake Death Toll Rises to 920; Humanitarian Crisis Deepens
summary: A powerful earthquake in Venezuela has killed approximately 920 people, with the death toll continuing to rise as rescue operations proceed. Families remain desperate for news of missing relatives. Pope Leo sent humanitarian aid to earthquake-struck Venezuela. Venezuela's interim president has vowed to respond to the disaster. The earthquake is one of the deadliest natural disasters in Venezuela's recent history.
exam_angle: Major natural disasters, their geographical context, and humanitarian response are relevant for GS 1 (Geography) and GS 2 (IR) topics on disaster management and international humanitarian law. Venezuela's location in South America and its seismic vulnerability are geographically testable.
key_facts: Venezuela earthquake death toll: approximately 920 (as of latest reports)
Described as one of Venezuela's deadliest recent natural disasters
Pope Leo (new Pope) sent humanitarian aid to Venezuela
Venezuela is located in northern South America, along the Caribbean plate boundary

title: Hyderabad Names Road 'Donald Trump Avenue'; US President Thanks India
summary: The city of Hyderabad unveiled a road named 'Donald Trump Avenue,' marking an unprecedented diplomatic gesture from an Indian city towards a sitting US President. Donald Trump personally thanked India for the honour. The gesture is seen as part of the broader India-US relationship building under the current political dispensation in both countries.
exam_angle: Symbolic diplomatic gestures between India and the US, the state of India-US bilateral relations, and people-to-people ties are relevant for GS 2 (IR). This connects to the broader India-US strategic partnership and the role of sub-national actors in diplomacy.
key_facts: Hyderabad named a road 'Donald Trump Avenue'
Trump personally thanked India for the honour
Hyderabad is the capital of Telangana and a major IT hub
Reflects India-US people-to-people and diplomatic ties

title: India-Switzerland Step Up Innovation Partnership with Focus on Startups and Research
summary: India and Switzerland have announced a strengthened innovation partnership focusing on startups, technology, and research collaboration. The partnership aims to facilitate bilateral cooperation in deep tech, sustainability, and digital innovation. Switzerland is home to major global financial institutions and research universities, making it a significant partner for India's innovation ecosystem.
exam_angle: Bilateral innovation partnerships, India's science diplomacy, and startup ecosystem development are relevant for GS 2 (IR) and GS 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech). India-Switzerland ties also connect to CERN collaboration and pharmaceutical sector interests.
key_facts: India and Switzerland strengthened innovation partnership
Focus areas: startups, technology, and research collaboration
Covers deep tech, sustainability, and digital innovation
Switzerland is a key partner for India in science diplomacy and pharmaceuticals
